summary.intent: Add Safari/Chrome-style URL autocomplete with Google suggestions, bookmarks, and history to the browser UI, while also fixing a button height mismatch and a silent navigation failure for bare domains like 'lemonde.fr'.
summary.outcome: Rewrote the NavigateBar component in page-browser.js with auto-prepend of https://, Google suggestions via suggestqueries.google.com, session URL history, keyboard navigation in dropdown, and matched button/input heights. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: Browser sessions created via UI default to sandbox mode when sandbox != 'none'; the service injects sandbox:true into requests missing it.
CSS class provider-btn-sm uses padding: 3px 10px; font-size: 0.75rem — used to match button height with input.
Google autocomplete suggestions fetched via suggestqueries.google.com with client=firefox for CORS-friendly JSON.
The project uses Tailwind; CSS rebuild is a separate step checked after JS changes.

code.finding: NavigateBar was fully rewritten to include https:// auto-prepend, res.success checking, Google suggestions dropdown with keyboard navigation, and session URL history.
provider-btn-sm class defined here; used to align Go button height with the URL input.

workflow: After JS changes, Tailwind must be explicitly rebuilt and checked for new utility classes — in this case no new classes were needed.
Committing from the repo root (not the worktree subdirectory) was required for the push to succeed.

friction: Navigation to bare domains like 'lemonde.fr' silently failed with no error — no https:// prepend and no success check on the response meant the page stayed on about:blank, appearing as 'Waiting for first frame'.
Button height mismatch was caused by padding class inconsistency between input and button elements.
Multiple grep attempts were needed to locate the provider-btn-sm class definition.
open_items: Bookmark integration in autocomplete suggestions was mentioned but not confirmed implemented — only history and Google suggestions are explicit in the transcript.
Favicon display in autocomplete dropdown was requested by user but not confirmed in the implementation details.
CORS behavior of suggestqueries.google.com with client=firefox should be validated in production — it's a workaround, not an official API.
